english: to be / to have been (past participle of sein)
notes: 'gewesen' is the past participle of 'sein'. It is used to form the Perfekt and Plusquamperfekt. As an auxiliary verb, 'sein' takes 'ist' in the Perfekt (not 'hat').

sentences.german: Er ist gestern in Berlin gewesen.
Sie war müde, weil sie die ganze Nacht wach gewesen war.
sentences.english: She was tired because she had been awake all night.

sentences.german: Das Wetter ist heute schön gewesen.
sentences.english: The weather has been nice today.
